Patch Set 1:

Thank you! I just tried this patch and it fixes our immediate issue with lctl ping on master.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

While you create a buffer based on IPv4. Say 4 is n_ids then its going to allocated with 32 bytes. But say only 3 ids coming back are IPv4 and one is IPv6 then you can have an overflow.

Patch Set 1:

James is correct, this change only works if there are no IPv6 NIDs. We still need to come up with a proper fix.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> James is correct, this change only works if there are no IPv6 NIDs. We still need to come up with a proper fix.

noted, thanks!

parents.subject: LU-17053 libcfs: make a debugfs equivalent for markers

commit.subject: LU-17700 lnet: fix lnet ping MD length calculation
commit.message:
LU-17700 lnet: fix lnet ping MD length calculation

LNet ping MD length calculation appears to have been broken.
Fix it.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-lnet
Fixes: c384a39d ("LU-10391 lnet: update ping to handle multiple NIDs")
Signed-off-by: Serguei Smirnov <ssmirnov@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I8da28acd015ef4574ce1cb8fdb2d0ac503766d37
